name: Ada's on the River

notes: Every frozen row was reconciled against Ada's current restaurant-issued structured dinner, seafood-bar, lunch, brunch, dessert, social-hour, and beverage menus, with the restaurant-linked Toast pickup surface used only as corroborating third-party evidence.

The corrected allergen-focused catalog contains 99 distinct current products across 18 real categories after consolidating duplicate meal-period presentations; it retains 89 positive published-signal rows and leaves 10 rows unavailable.

The frozen 49-row pickup subset retained 45 current or renamed products, three stale kids products, and one optional sauce incorrectly flattened as a standalone item. It omitted 54 current distinct products, collapsed every category to American, and repeatedly shifted neighboring product names into variantGroup.
Alcohol-only cocktails, beer, wine, and whiskey remain outside this allergen-focused catalog. Six zero-proof products and Heineken 0.0 are retained after all food and dessert rows.

Ada's publishes current item descriptions and defines V as vegetarian that may include egg or dairy and G as gluten free, but it publishes no complete allergen matrix, complete recipes, or cross-contact guide.

V and G are not converted into positive allergen claims. Positive fields come only from fixed published ingredients, named fish or shellfish species, and mandatory food formats; optional salad proteins, substitutions, and modifier sauces are excluded.

Coconut is not promoted to tree nut. The asparagus therefore carries fixed milk and egg from white-chocolate béarnaise, not the frozen tree-nut claim. Ginger beer is not treated as gluten-bearing beer.
Twenty-five of the 45 surviving frozen rows had incorrect allergen fields: 22 underreported positive signals, two were mixed over/understatements, and one overreported an unsupported signal.
The frozen generic Toast extraction was stale and flattened, used a fake American category, and populated variantGroup from adjacent product names rather than real option semantics.

The official website's current JavaScript loader fetches the retained /wp-json/api/v1/get_menus endpoint. The correction parses that restaurant-issued object, filters meal sections explicitly, preserves fixed section context, excludes alcohol and optional sauces, consolidates known duplicate meal presentations, and keeps beverages last.
The parser asserts raw menu-period counts before producing the compact catalog so a future source-shape or scope change fails focused verification instead of silently changing data.
The final-stage repair replaces Ada's only when verified count, category count, stale-row exclusions, sentinel allergen semantics, unavailable-row behavior, or beverage order regress.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Confirmed the Alexandria restaurant identity and current in-page menu UI; retained the official page and its menu-loader JavaScript, which identifies the restaurant-issued WordPress JSON endpoint.

Captured and reviewed the current structured payload: 378 total menu rows across all surfaces and 155 in the food, dessert, social-bite, zero-proof, and 0.0-beer scope, producing 99 distinct current products after documented consolidation.
notes: The endpoint is restaurant-issued and is the data source rendered by the official website.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Direct automated retrieval returned HTTP 403. The retained render-proxy artifact corroborates the current pickup identity and a smaller current dinner-oriented subset, but was not promoted to official authority.
notes: The restaurant-issued structured endpoint, not the proxy, is authoritative for corrected menu and ingredient text.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Full-text review of the current official page, loader, structured menus, and linked pickup surface found menu descriptions and the V/G legends but no complete allergen matrix, complete ingredient guide, or cross-contact policy.

findings.summary: The frozen catalog contained only 49 flattened pickup rows in a fake American category, retained three stale kids rows and an optional sauce as a product, and omitted 54 distinct current products.
findings.resolution: Rebuilt 99 distinct current products across 18 real categories, removed stale kids rows and the modifier artifact, consolidated duplicate meal presentations, and placed retained nonalcoholic beverages last.

findings.summary: Frozen variantGroup values repeatedly contained adjacent product names, demonstrating broken row association rather than real option groups.
findings.resolution: Restored current restaurant-issued categories and source contexts and preserved configurability only where the structured source actually publishes cost options.

findings.summary: Twenty-five of 45 surviving frozen products had incorrect allergen fields, principally omitted shellfish, fish, egg, milk, soy, mustard, wheat, or gluten signals, with two mixed errors and one unsupported claim.
findings.resolution: Re-derived only fixed positive signals from current published descriptions, named species, and mandatory formats; kept V/G labels and optional choices out of positive fields and left ten unsupported current products unavailable.

findings.summary: The official website currently renders a comprehensive restaurant-issued JSON menu, while the frozen output relied on a smaller stale Toast extraction and lost most categories and products.
findings.resolution: Made the official website's current structured menu the deterministic correction source and retained Toast only as corroborating third-party evidence.

repairs.summary: Prepared and targeted-tested a 99-item current Ada's catalog spanning current food, dessert, social-hour, and nonalcoholic surfaces with source-correct positive allergen semantics and beverages last.
verificationCommands: RESTAURANT_REPOSITORY_PATH=/tmp/adas-river-repository.json node scripts/repair-final-generated-regressions.mjs (isolated copy; verified 99 unique rows, 18 categories, 89 official-ingredient rows, 10 unavailable rows, no stale kids rows or standalone House Steak Sauce, correct sentinel allergen semantics, and beverages last)

notes: Current corrected catalog counts: 99 items, 18 categories, 89 positive published-signal rows, and 10 accurately unavailable rows.

Focused source-shape, scope-count, compact-deduplication, all 49 frozen-row reconciliation, category, unique-id, optional-choice, V/G legend, coconut, mandatory-format, beverage-scope, and beverage-order checks passed.
